old_claim.note: Asteroid Industrial Ecosystems is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: Asteroid mining supplies the excavation, cavity creation, and in-situ resource access foundation for habitat caverns inside mined asteroids.

source_shape.source_locator: The paper's abstract frames the concept around caverns of mined asteroids and investigates housing a habitat inside an entire mined asteroid.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Asteroid habitat caverns are directly tied to mined asteroids and excavation/cavity creation, not to a full asteroid industrial ecosystem.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge required a broad linked mining, refining, manufacturing, propellant, habitat, and logistics ecosystem; the source supports the narrower mining and excavation foundation.

ontology_before: The graph made asteroid habitat caverns depend on full asteroid industrial ecosystems, implying broad orbital supply-chain and manufacturing infrastructure is prerequisite to the habitat concept.
ontology_after: The graph routes asteroid habitat caverns through asteroid mining, radiation shielding, and closed-loop life support, keeping industrial ecosystems as a broader downstream scaling concept.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: asteroid_habitat_caverns now depends on asteroid_mining and no longer directly on asteroid_industrial_ecosystems.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to full asteroid-city industrial networks rather than mined asteroid habitat caverns.
A source shows asteroid habitat caverns specifically require the entire asteroid industrial ecosystem, not just mining/excavation, shielding, and life support.
A downstream node requires a full industrial asteroid settlement and should point to asteroid_industrial_ecosystems separately.
short_reason: Asteroid mining is the direct excavation and resource-access prerequisite; asteroid industrial ecosystems were too broad for the cavern habitat concept.
